_comment: Source of truth for the M7-TOOLCHAIN acceptance suite scaffolding (issue #423). Each entry pairs an acceptance marker from plans/2026-05-28-in-os-toolchain-self-hosting.md with the gating execute issue whose landing flips its SKIP to PASS. Consumed by build/scripts/validate_bundle.sh to emit the m7_toolchain section of the bundle JSON report.

markers.name: toolchain_compiles_hello_in_os
markers.description: cc driver app produces a SOF-wrapped ELF on-target.

markers.name: toolchain_runs_compiled_binary
markers.description: End-to-end run of cc output: os_process_spawn (#422) merged via PR #427; remaining gates are the cc driver (#409) producing a binary on-target and the launcher unsigned-run wiring (#410) that actually executes it.

markers.name: toolchain_unsigned_prompt_enforced
markers.description: Unsigned-run wiring through the launcher auth flow (Tier 1 trust, AUTH_TYPE_UNSIGNED_BIN).

markers.name: toolchain_large_output_persisted
markers.description: cc emits a >1 KB binary; FS path (M7-TOOLCHAIN-002, closed #405) stays byte-identical.

markers.name: toolchain_compile_error_reported
markers.description: cc exits non-zero on syntax error, prints a diagnostic, and writes no output file.

markers.name: toolchain_cc_manifest_sidecar_written_on_link
markers.description: When `cc` links with no `--manifest` and no pre-existing sidecar, it synthesises a manifest via libmanifestgen and writes `<output>.manifest.json`.

markers.name: toolchain_cc_version_and_help_text_pinned
markers.description: Pre-#409 SKIP-pinned golden contract for `cc --version` and `cc --help` stdout bytes, exit code 0, and empty stderr.

markers.name: toolchain_heap_isolation
markers.description: Two sequential `cc` runs in one boot must not see each other's arena state. Kernel half (os_mem_brk + per-process arena reset) landed: syscall + native-bridge slot in #421 via PR #432 (af8ece8), clib_os_brk forwarder via PR #455 (30d6443), launcher per-spawn runtime.arena_bytes enforcement via PR #454 (d6ae05a, closes #448). Remaining gates are the `cc` driver app (#409) that lets us actually invoke `cc` twice in one boot and the unsigned-run/acceptance-suite wiring tracked by #410.

markers.name: toolchain_launch_audit_owner_kind_field_emitted
markers.description: Pre-#410 SKIP pin for launch audit records: both `launch.granted` and `launch.denied` lines must carry `owner_kind=<internal|external|local>` per docs/abi/audit-markers.md and issue #554.

markers.name: toolchain_libc_deps_phase3_complete
markers.description: TinyCC Phase-3 libc-deps completion marker. Stays SKIP while either #538 (POSIX-fd nucleus) or #539 (remaining 11 gaps) is OPEN; must flip off awaiting_* once both close.
